steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content:
Left side: Writers... but many are still well ____ — an independent clause
Right side: Koulsy Lamko, for example, is a celebrated playwright from Chad — an independent clause

steps.title: Apply the independent-clause separation rule
steps.content:
A colon separates two independent clauses when the second elaborates or exemplifies the first — exactly what's needed when “for example” signals Koulsy Lamko exemplifies the preceding claim.

The other choices violate sentence-joining conventions: no punctuation (A) creates a run-on, a comma alone (D) creates a comma splice, and “and” (C) produces an ungrammatical construction.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
